title: Payment failures on Saturday and Sunday evenings
description: Customers reporting payment failures on weekend evenings. Failure rate ~15% of transactions. Started approximately 3 weeks ago. Affects both new and returning customers. No errors in our logs — Stripe is returning success but transfers don't appear.
resolution: Added retry logic with 7-second delay between attempts after investigating with Stripe support. Root cause: Stripe has an undocumented rate limit that is lower on weekends. Resolution is a workaround, not a fix.

title: Stripe cascade failure during retry storm
description: Settlement failed for approximately 4 hours on March 15. All retries fired simultaneously when Stripe rate-limited us, amplifying the load. Took down settlement for the full afternoon. Finance has flagged this as a P0 recurring risk.
resolution: Increased STRIPE_RETRY_DELAY from 2 seconds to 7 seconds to break up the retry wave. Added comment warning against changing this value.

title: Settlement job missing clearing deadline
description: Daily settlement did not complete before 6 AM UTC clearing deadline on August 16. Approximately 2,000 merchants did not receive same-day settlement. Finance is fielding complaints. This is the first time we have missed the deadline since launch.
resolution: Reduced batch size from 100 to 50 and added 2-second sleep between batches after discovering undocumented rate limit on processor API. Follow-up ticket SET-118 to tune parameters.

title: Tune settlement batch parameters after August incident
description: Batch size of 50 and 2.0s sleep from emergency fix (SET-112) may not be optimal. Need to find the right parameters to maximize throughput while staying within rate limits. Must not miss the 6 AM deadline.
resolution: After testing: batch size 47 with 2.1s sleep is the maximum throughput that stays reliably under the processor rate limit. The 0.1s extra on sleep accounts for retry overhead. Parameters set in post-mortem follow-up.

title: Mass logout event during session token migration
description: During migration from sha256 to bcrypt session tokens, all users were logged out simultaneously when we invalidated the old token format. Mobile users were disproportionately affected because the mobile app does not handle re-auth gracefully — it crashes on 401.
resolution: Added 24-hour grace period for legacy tokens to avoid repeat mass logout. This extends legacy session duration beyond intended. Not ideal but prevents customer impact. Mobile team to fix their 401 handling.

title: Compromised admin account — 6 hour window
description: Admin account (user ID 4829) credentials were compromised and used to access PII for 6 hours before detection. Security team flagged that our session invalidation was all-or-nothing — we could not invalidate just admin sessions without logging out all users.
resolution: Added invalidate_admin_sessions_for_user() for targeted session invalidation. Only handles admin sessions. Standard sessions not included in scope — broader invalidation was causing collateral issues in testing.

title: Legacy merchant migration to Stripe blocked by KYC
description: Attempting to migrate 2,300 legacy merchants from Braintree to Stripe as part of platform consolidation. Stripe's KYC requirements are stricter than Braintree's. Estimate 15-20% of legacy merchants would fail Stripe's automated KYC checks.
resolution: Migration cancelled indefinitely. Legal signed off on keeping legacy merchants on Braintree. LEGACY_MERCHANT_CUTOFF timestamp is now permanent. Braintree contract extended.

title: Stripe returning HTTP 200 with error body for prepaid cards
description: Certain prepaid card types cause Stripe to return an HTTP 200 response with an error object in the body instead of a 4xx. This means our standard error handling does not catch these failures — they silently succeed from our perspective but the payment does not go through.
resolution: Added explicit check for error body in _call_stripe(). Stripe confirmed this is a known issue in their internal tracker but it is not on their public roadmap to fix. Our workaround is the only option.
